proposal.reasoning: We are in the first exploration phase with no prior data, so we need two contrasting forks to sample opposite regions of the parameter space. hypothesis.scientific_rationale: Increasing the dummy‑core bond lengths (r₀) and the magnitude of opposite charges (q) directly enlarges the separation of opposite partial charges, which raises the molecular dipole moment (hypothesis: larger r₀ → larger dipole). Strengthening the Lennard‑Jones ε (more negative) and shrinking σ (rmin) enhances cohesive forces, leading to tighter packing, higher density, higher ΔH_vap and higher surface tension. Conversely, reducing these parameters should lower cohesion and dipole strength, providing a contrasting reference.
hypothesis.expected_benefit: Fork 1 should move the simulated dipole moment toward the experimental 3.96 D and increase density/surface tension toward the target values. Fork 2 explores the opposite extreme, helping the optimizer understand how much each parameter influences the properties and avoiding premature convergence.
hypothesis.test_method: The two parameter sets will be run in parallel simulations; their resulting thermodynamic and electronic observables will be compared against the target data to validate the hypothesis.
